ead.archdesc:

English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Ithaca and Tompkins County (N.Y.) printed material
no primary creator
Kahn, Peter, 1921-1997.
Conley, Edward J.
Korf, Richard P.
Reps, John William.
Harbin, Terry.
1074
2.4 cubic feet. 2.4 cubic feet.
1858-1992. Photographs, maps, brochures, booklets, programs, broadsides, invitations Included are photographs, maps, brochures, booklets, programs, broadsides, invitations, historical outlines, and other printed items pertaining to subjects including local politics and government, urban renewal, religion, civic and social events and organizations, musical and dramatic productions, transportation, educational institutions, and geology of the region. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 ======= 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2090027
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Sarah E. Keating
Date completed:
June 2004
EAD encoding:
Sarah E. Keating, May 2004Peter Martinez, June 2004
Date Modified:
Jude Corina, January 2017
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Included are photographs, maps, brochures, booklets, programs, broadsides, invitations, historical outlines, and other printed items pertaining to subjects including local politics and government, urban renewal, religion, civic and social events and organizations, musical and dramatic productions, transportation, educational institutions, and geology of the region.
Broadside: "REWARD - Who Killed the Harris Family?...," 1990.
"Ithaca, New York: A General Plan" (1971) with sketches by Peter Kahn; members of the Planning Board include Edward J. Conley, Richard P. Korf, and John W. Reps. Also, environmental impact statements prepared for the Strand Theatre, 1992.
Poster relating to Wharton Studios and the 75th anniversary of movie making in Ithaca, produced by Terry Harbin, 1990
Ithaca Sign Company banner.
RELATED MATERIALS
Includes collections #1074, #1158, 1214, 1219, 1861, 1864, 1872, 1897, 1915, and 2652.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Ithaca Government
Series II. Ithaca Schools, Ithaca College and Cornell University
Series III. Ithaca Businesses
Series IV. Ithaca Churches
Series V. Ithaca Organizations
Series VI. Ithaca Resources
Series VII. Miscellaneous
Cite As:
Ithaca and Tompkins County (N.Y.) Printed Material, #1074. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Posters. Pamphlets. Photographs. Broadsides. Maps. Tompkins County (N.Y.) -- Commerce. Tompkins County (N.Y.) -- Politics and government. Tompkins County (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs. Tompkins County (N.Y.) -- Social life and customs. Tompkins County (N.Y.) -- Pictorial works. Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs. Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Pictorial works. Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Social life and customs. Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Politics and government. Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Commerce. Ithaca (N.Y.) -- History. Motion picture studios -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Theaters -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Schools -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Religious institutions -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Murder -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Churches -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Wharton Studio (Ithaca, N.Y.)
